title: Pfizer — Projects
html:
Pfizer Active Projects & Workstream Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Pfizer account has 10+ project engagements across vaccines and two emerging non-vaccine footholds, with the last 12 months showing clear acceleration — from a 2-brand HCPP/PET operation to a diversifying portfolio that now includes ATU, Digital Tracking, Message Labs, a pharmacist quant study, and a first oncology PET. Three strategic themes cut across the project portfolio: (1) HCP-Patient Conversations is the anchor capability that creates trust and upsell pathways on every brand; (2) the insights produced are delivering portfolio-level patterns (communication style, brand-agnostic recommendations, disease severity gaps) that elevate ZoomRx from brand-level vendor to strategic partner; and (3) the non-vaccine projects (Message Labs, Lorbrena PET, Pharmacist Quant) represent the early diversification moves that will determine whether this account crosses $700K in FY27.

Account Health Dashboard

Project Brand Service Status Key Insight
PFZ_Pharms Quant (#17) Vaccines (cross) Quant :green_circle: Active Most recent project (Apr 2026); pharmacist channel study
PFZ_Message Labs (#16) Above-brand (AI Migraine) Message Testing :blue_circle: New AI Migraine Messaging Pilot with Aimee Renaud; first non-vaccine project
PFZ_Meningococcal_ATU (#15) Trumenba/Penbraya ATU :green_circle: Active First ATU at Pfizer
PFZ_PREVNAR_HCP-Pt 2025 (#14) Prevnar Adult HCPP :green_circle: Active Signed FY26; targeting 50-64 age group
PFZ_LORBRENA_PET (#13) Lorbrena PET :green_circle: Active First oncology project at Pfizer
PFZ_ABRYSVO_Maternal_HCP-Pt Study (#12) Abrysvo Maternal HCPP :green_circle: Recurring Repeat engagement
PFZ_Meningococcal_HCP-Pt Study (#11) Trumenba/Penbraya HCPP :green_circle: Active Established HCPP engagement
PFZ_Respiratory_Vaccine_Pharmacist Pulse (#10) Vaccines (cross) Pulse Survey :white_circle: Completed Pharmacist pain points across vaccine portfolio
PFZ_ABRYSVO_OA_HCP-Pt Study (#09) Abrysvo OA HCPP :white_circle: Completed n=10; revealed brand-agnostic recommendation pattern
PFZ_ABRYSVO_Maternal_Digital Tracking (#08) Abrysvo Maternal Digital Tracking :green_circle: Active Digital patient journey tracking

Additional active/recent:

  • Abrysvo Maternal PET (recurring) — multiple waves of promotional effectiveness tracking
  • Abrysvo OA PET — signed for FY26 ($75K)
  • Pneumococcal HCP-Pt Final Report v2 (Jan 2026) — the most insight-rich deliverable on the account

Legend: :green_circle: Active/On Track | :blue_circle: New (recently launched) | :white_circle: Completed | :yellow_circle: Watch | :red_circle: Risk

Detailed Workstream Profiles

HCP-Patient Conversations (HCPP)

Pneumococcal HCP-Pt Conversations (Prevnar 20)

Status: Completed | Latest: Final Report v2 (Jan 2026) | Sample: n=20

What's working:

  • Most commercially actionable study on the account — portfolio-level behavioral findings
  • Assertive/presumptive communication style = 100% acceptance vs. ~60% with suggestive style
  • Prevnar 20 holds strongest HCP mind-share: recommended by name in 12/20 conversations

Key findings:

  • "One and done" messaging is the single most effective acceptance driver
  • Serotype counting risk: when both PCV20 and PCV21 are discussed, Capvaxive wins 3/7 conversations
  • Pharmacy referral is a major drop-off point (4/20 patients referred out; in-office stock = 75% same-day acceptance)
  • Disease severity systematically under-communicated — HCPs position pneumonia as manageable, not life-threatening
  • 5-year re-dosing misconception persists among 3/5 brand-agnostic HCPs

Follow-up opportunities:

  • Capvaxive competitive tracking study (PET or conversation-based) — quantify serotype counting prevalence
  • 50-64 age group messaging optimization study — ACIP expansion creates new patient segment
  • Pharmacist channel HCP-Pt conversations — what happens after the referral?

Contact: Tim Douglas / Lisa Godek

Abrysvo OA HCP-Pt Conversations (RSV Adults)

Status: Completed | Latest: Full Report (Nov-Dec 2024) | Sample: n=10

What's working:

  • Revealed the brand-agnostic recommendation pattern — 8/10 HCPs did not name a specific RSV vaccine brand
  • Identified disease severity under-communication (7/10 low/no severity discussion)

Key findings:

  • Only 2/10 HCPs made brand-specific recommendations (one Arexvy, one mResvia — neither Abrysvo)
  • Efficacy data is NOT discussed with patients; HCPs focus on dosing, side effects, CDC recommendations
  • HCPs who stock a vaccine recommend it but without naming the brand
  • Patients do not ask about vaccine efficacy — questions center on side effects, administration, insurance

Follow-up opportunities:

  • RSV brand differentiation study — what would make HCPs name Abrysvo specifically?
  • Cross-portfolio vaccine communication training study — leveraging findings across RSV + pneumococcal

Contact: Antonietta Forte

Abrysvo Maternal HCP-Pt Study

Status: Active (recurring) | Latest: Repeat engagement 2026

Recurring HCPP engagement on ZoomRx's anchor brand at Pfizer. Abrysvo Maternal faces unique competitive dynamics — nirsevimab (Beyfortus, Sanofi/AZ) is winning the infant RSV immunization battle in the US (38%+ coverage via nirsevimab vs. 19% via maternal vaccination).

Contact: Tim Douglas (formerly Lisa Dixon)

Meningococcal HCP-Pt Study (Trumenba/Penbraya)

Status: Active | Contact: Gabby Gonzalez

HCPP on meningococcal vaccination. Penbraya's value proposition is "fewer shots" (2-dose pentavalent vs. 4-5 separate injections), but the underlying challenge may be MenB apathy (~30% coverage among 17-year-olds) rather than dosing complexity.

Follow-up opportunities:

  • MenB awareness/perceived necessity study
  • Digital Tracking (in active discussion with Gabby)
  • PET pilot (planned for Q2-Q3 FY26)

Prevnar Adult HCP-Pt 2025

Status: Active | Signed: FY26 | Contact: Tim Douglas / Lisa Godek

Targeting the newly expanded 50-64 age group following the October 2024 ACIP recommendation for all adults 50+. This is a strategically important study — the ACIP expansion added ~30 million vaccine-eligible adults, and Pfizer needs to understand how to message to this younger cohort.

PET / Promotional Effectiveness Tracking

Abrysvo Maternal PET

Status: Active (recurring) | Revenue: ~$150K | Contact: Tim Douglas

The highest-revenue single project on the account. Multiple waves of quantitative tracking measuring rep effectiveness, message recall, and prescribing intent for Abrysvo RSV maternal indication. AI-enhanced analytics on this PET established ZoomRx's AI pioneer reputation at Pfizer.

Watch items:

  • Seasonal concentration — RSV vaccination window (August-January) compresses research activity
  • Nirsevimab competition may reduce Abrysvo Maternal market relevance over time (Thesis 2)

Abrysvo OA PET

Status: Active | Revenue: ~$75K | Contact: Antonietta Forte

PET signed for FY26 tracking Abrysvo promotional effectiveness in the older adult RSV market. The narrowed CDC recommendation (75+ universal; 50-74 at increased risk) constrains the addressable market.

Lorbrena PET (#13)

Status: Active | Contact: [Populate]

Strategic significance: This is ZoomRx's first oncology project at Pfizer. Lorbrena (lorlatinib) treats ALK-positive metastatic NSCLC — a targeted oncology niche. While the project itself may be small, it establishes a foothold in Pfizer's oncology organization (heavily expanded by the Seagen acquisition). This could open paths to Padcev, Adcetris, Xtandi, and Ibrance.

[Populate project details, contact, and status from project team.]

ATU / Market Tracking

Meningococcal ATU (#15)

Status: Active | Contact: Gabby Gonzalez

First ATU study at Pfizer. Tracks awareness, trial, and usage for meningococcal vaccines among HCPs. Sermo was the previous ATU incumbent — this engagement represents a competitive displacement.

Strategic significance: Proves ATU can be delivered at Pfizer. If successful, replicable to Abrysvo Maternal (displacing Sermo on the anchor brand) and Prevnar Adult.

Digital & Channel Tracking

Abrysvo Maternal Digital Tracking (#08)

Status: Active | Contact: Tim Douglas

Monitoring the digital patient journey around Abrysvo Maternal RSV vaccination. Part of the multi-service stack on ZoomRx's anchor brand (PET + HCPP + Digital Tracking).

Follow-up opportunity: Trumenba/Penbraya Digital Tracking is in active discussion with Gabby Gonzalez and nearly closed.

Pharmacist & Cross-Portfolio Research

Pharmacist Pain Points Pulse Survey (#10)

Status: Completed | Contact: [Populate]

Cross-portfolio pharmacist research exploring pain points in the pharmacy channel for RSV, pneumococcal, and meningococcal vaccination. Addresses the pharmacy referral drop-off risk identified in multiple HCPP studies.

Pharms Quant (#17)

Status: Active (Apr 2026) | Contact: [Populate]

Most recent project on the account. Quantitative study targeting pharmacists. Represents continued investment in understanding the pharmacy channel — a critical distribution point for adult vaccines.

Message Testing & AI Pilots

Message Labs — AI Migraine Messaging Pilot (#16)

Status: New | Contact: Aimee Renaud (AI Excellence)

Strategic significance: Highest-priority project on the account.

This is ZoomRx's first non-vaccine project at Pfizer, commissioned by Aimee Renaud in Pfizer's AI Excellence team. It tests AI-powered message optimization methodology in the migraine space (Nurtec context). The project matters far more than its revenue because:

  1. It proves ZoomRx capabilities beyond vaccines — breaking the "vaccine HCPP vendor" pigeonhole
  2. It deepens the relationship with Aimee Renaud, the only above-brand contact and the key referral path into non-vaccine brands
  3. It creates an AI capabilities proof point that can be referenced in any future Pfizer pitch
  4. It directly connects to the "$125K other brands" FY26 revenue target

Watch items:

  • Delivery quality must be exceptional — this is an audition for the entire non-vaccine expansion
  • Capture Aimee Renaud's feedback and advocacy explicitly after delivery
  • Use results to request intros to Nurtec, Xeljanz, and Vyndaquel teams

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. HCPP Is the Platform — Everything Else Builds on It

HCP-Patient Conversations is not just a service; it is the strategic foundation of the Pfizer relationship. Every PET upsell, every ATU displacement, and every new brand entry traces back to HCPP results creating trust and credibility. The specific insights generated (communication style, brand-agnostic recommendations, disease severity gaps, pharmacy drop-off) are cited in upsell conversations and differentiate ZoomRx from competitors who provide data without actionable recommendations.

Implication: HCPP delivery quality must remain consistently exceptional. Any dip in output quality on HCPP risks the entire upsell pipeline.

2. Portfolio-Level Findings Elevate ZoomRx's Strategic Value

The cross-study patterns documented in the synthesis (assertive communication = higher acceptance, brand-agnostic recommendations dominate, disease severity under-communicated, pharmacy referral drop-off) apply across the entire Pfizer vaccine portfolio — and potentially beyond vaccines. These portfolio-level findings position ZoomRx as a strategic insights partner rather than a brand-level research vendor.

Implication: Present cross-portfolio findings proactively, even to brand teams who didn't commission them. Tim Douglas should hear the RSV findings; Antonietta Forte should hear the pneumococcal findings. This creates the "connecting the dots" value that brand-independent research cannot deliver.

3. The Non-Vaccine Frontier Will Define FY27

Lorbrena PET and Message Labs AI Pilot are the first two steps outside vaccines. If both succeed, the path to Paxlovid, Nurtec, Xeljanz, and potentially oncology brands opens. If either disappoints, non-vaccine expansion stalls and the account remains a $400-500K vaccines-only engagement.

Implication: Treat these two projects as high-touch, principal-visibility engagements. Ensure Rich Durante and Nick Fleischman are briefing Vinoth on their progress.

4. Competitive Displacement Opportunities Are Real but Require Patience

Sermo (ATU), Verilogue (HCPP on Prevnar Peds), and BI (PETs) are all incumbent competitors. The Meningococcal ATU already represents a Sermo displacement. The Q2 zero-cost PET pilot plan targets BI. Verilogue displacement on Prevnar Peds depends on Abrysvo Maternal HCPP results convincing Stephanie Archer.

Implication: Track each displacement attempt explicitly. Win rates matter — successfully displacing one incumbent creates momentum; failing creates doubt. Prioritize the displacement with the highest probability of success (Sermo ATU, already in progress) over the hardest (BI PET).

Immediate Priorities

  1. Monitor Message Labs delivery quality. This is the most strategically important project on the account. Exceptional delivery opens non-vaccine expansion; mediocre delivery closes it. Ensure the team knows the stakes.

  2. Close Trumenba Digital Tracking. Gabby Gonzalez is in active discussion. This is the lowest-effort revenue opportunity on the account (~$50K, nearly closed). Push to close.

  3. Prepare Prevnar Adult HCPP results for Tim Douglas. Strong results here create the upsell path to ATU ($50-75K) and PET pilot on Prevnar Adult. The quality of this deliverable directly impacts $125K+ in pipeline.

  4. Track Lorbrena PET progress. As the first oncology project, it deserves visibility even if small. Understand who the Pfizer oncology contact is and whether there's appetite for more.

  5. Plan Q2 PET pilot. The AO plan calls for a zero-cost PET pilot on Trumenba or Prevnar Adult in Q2. Confirm which brand, timeline, and resourcing.

  6. Schedule Paxlovid HCPP pitch. Gavin Hansen is the warm contact for the highest-priority "other brand" entry. Bring the Abrysvo HCPP case study. [Check with Rich Durante on timing.]

Project Timeline (Last 12 Months)

# Project Date Service Brand
17 PFZ_Pharms Quant Apr 2026 Quant Vaccines (cross)
16 PFZ_Message Labs 2026 Message Testing Above-brand (AI Migraine)
15 PFZ_Meningococcal_ATU 2026 ATU Trumenba/Penbraya
14 PFZ_PREVNAR_HCP-Pt 2025 2025-2026 HCPP Prevnar Adult
13 PFZ_LORBRENA_PET 2025-2026 PET Lorbrena
12 PFZ_ABRYSVO_Maternal_HCP-Pt Study 2025-2026 HCPP Abrysvo Maternal
11 PFZ_Meningococcal_HCP-Pt Study 2025 HCPP Trumenba/Penbraya
10 PFZ_Respiratory_Vaccine_Pharmacist Pulse 2025 Pulse Survey Vaccines (cross)
09 PFZ_ABRYSVO_OA_HCP-Pt Study 2024-2025 HCPP Abrysvo OA
08 PFZ_ABRYSVO_Maternal_Digital Tracking 2024-2025 Digital Tracking Abrysvo Maternal

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
